paper_title: Sediment Lipid and Isotope Analysis Methods

methods.description: Process for extracting lipids from dried sediment samples and separating them into neutral and acid fractions, followed by further processing of each fraction.

steps.description: Lipids were extracted (×3) from ∼1 g of dried sediment using a Dionex ASE 200 Accelerated Solvent Extractor (Dionex, Sunnyvale, CA, United States) with dichloromethane:methanol (6:4).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform accelerated solvent extraction of lipids from dried sediment

steps.description: The extract was separated into neutral and acid fractions via aminopropyl silica gel column chromatography (Gao et al., 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Separate lipid extract into neutral and acid fractions using aminopropyl silica gel column chromatography

steps.description: The acid fraction was methylated with methanol:acetyl chloride (95:5) and purified with SiO 2 column chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: Methylate the acid fraction and purify using SiO2 column chromatography

steps.description: The neutral fraction was separated into four fractions using SiO 2 column chromatography: F1, 3 ml hexane; F2, 3 ml hexane:toluene (3:1); F3, 4 ml toluene; and F4, 3 ml toluene:CH 3 OH (3:1).
steps.searchable_summary: Separate the neutral fraction into four sub-fractions (F1-F4) using SiO2 column chromatography with specified solvent mixtures

methods.description: Analysis of the concentration of n-fatty acids in the purified acid fraction using Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detection.

steps.description: Samples were dissolved in toluene.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve purified methylated acid fraction sample in toluene

steps.description: The concentration of n-fatty acids in the acid fraction was analyzed using gas chromatography (GC) with an Agilent 6890 series gas chromatograph with on-column injection and electronic pressure control systems, and a flame ionization detector (Agilent, Santa Clara, CA, United States). Helium was the carrier gas and the flow velocity was maintained at 30 cm/s. An Agilent J&W CP-Sil 5 CB column was used (length 50 m; i.d. 0.25 mm; thickness 0.25 μm). The oven temperature was programmed from 100 to 130°C at 20°C/min and from 130 to 310°C at 4°C/min, and then maintained at 310°C for 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sample solution using GC-FID with specified column and temperature program
inputs: Temperature program (100-130°C at 20°C/min, 130-310°C at 4°C/min, hold 310°C for 30 min)

steps.description: Quantification was achieved by integrating the peak area...
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate peaks in GC chromatograms and quantify n-fatty acid concentrations

methods.description: Analysis of the δ13C of methylated n-fatty acids in the acid fraction using Gas Chromatography coupled with Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometry.

steps.description: The sample was dissolved in toluene.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve purified methylated acid fraction sample in toluene

steps.description: The δ 13 C (‰ Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite [VPDB]) of the methylated n-fatty acids in the acid fraction was analyzed using a Thermo Fisher Scientific (Waltham, MA, United States) GC IsoLink II with a capillary column coated with a TG-5MS column (30 m length; i.d. 0.25 mm; 0.25 μm film thickness) combined with a Delta V mass spectrometer through a combustion furnace at 1,000°C. The oven temperature was programmed from 100 to 230°C at 30°C/min and from 230 to 310°C at 4°C/min and then maintained at 310°C for 15 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sample solution using GC-IRMS with specified column, temperature program, and combustion furnace
inputs: Thermo Fisher Scientific GC IsoLink II
Temperature program (100-230°C at 30°C/min, 230-310°C at 4°C/min, hold 310°C for 15 min)

steps.description: As an internal isotopic standard, n-C 36 H 74 was used to check the measurement conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Use n-C36H74 as an internal isotopic standard to check measurement conditions

steps.description: Data were converted into values relative to VPDB using standard delta notation by comparison with CO 2 standard gas.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert raw isotopic data into δ13C values relative to VPDB using standard delta notation and comparison with CO2 standard gas

steps.description: The δ 13 C values of fatty acids were obtained from the measured values of fatty acid-methyl esters by correcting for methyl carbon (−34.1‰).
steps.searchable_summary: Correct the δ13C values of fatty acids for the methyl carbon contribution
outputs: Corrected δ13C values for fatty acids

steps.description: The reproducibility of the measurement based on repeated analyses was better than ±0.1‰.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess the reproducibility of the measurement based on repeated analyses

methods.description: Analysis of Glycerol Dialkyl Glycerol Tetraethers (GDGTs) from the neutral fraction (specifically F4) using High-Performance Liquid Chromatography coupled with Mass Spectrometry.

steps.description: An aliquot of F4 was dissolved in hexane-2-propanol (99:1), spiked with an internal standard (500 ng of C 46 glycerol trialkyl glycerol tetraether [GTGT]; Patwardhan and Thompson, 1999), and filtered through a short bed of Na 2 SO 4 .
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve aliquot of neutral fraction F4 in solvent, spike with internal standard, and filter
outputs: Filtered, spiked sample solution for HPLC-MS

steps.description: GDGTs were analyzed using highperformance liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (HPLC-MS) with an Agilent 1260 HPLC system coupled to an Agilent 6130 Series quadrupole mass spectrometer. Separation was achieved on two ultra-HPLC silica columns (ACQUITY BEH HILIC columns, 2.1 × 150 mm, 1.7 μm; Waters, Milford, MA, United States) in series, fitted with a 2.1 × 5 m pre-column of the same material (Waters) and maintained at 30°C. GDGTs were eluted isocratically for 25 min with 18% B, followed by a linear gradient to 35% B in 25 min, then a linear gradient to 100% B in 30 min, where A was hexane and B was hexane:isopropanol (9:1, v/v). The flow rate was 0.2 ml/min. The total run time was 90 min with a 20 min re-equilibration. Ionization was achieved using atmospheric pressure, positive ion chemical ionization-MS. The spectrometer was run in selected ion monitoring mode (m/z 743.8, 1,018, 1,020, 1,022, 1,032, 1,034, 1,036, 1,046, 1,048, 1,050, 1,292.3, 1,296.3, 1,298.3, 1,300.3, and 1,302.3).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze filtered sample solution using HPLC-MS with specified columns, temperature, gradient program, and MS detection mode (SIM)
inputs: ACQUITY BEH HILIC columns (2.1 × 150 mm, 1.7 μm)
Gradient program (A=hexane, B=hexane:isopropanol 9:1; 18% B for 25 min, to 35% B in 25 min, to 100% B in 30 min)
MS selected ion monitoring mode (m/z values)

steps.description: Compounds were identified by comparing mass spectra and retention times with those in the literature (Hopmans et al., 2000;De Jonge et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify compounds by comparing mass spectra and retention times with literature data

steps.description: Quantification was achieved by integrating the peak area in the (M+H) + chromatogram and comparing these with the peak area of an internal standard (C 46 GTGT) in the (M+H) + chromatogram according to the method of Huguet et al. (2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify GDGT compounds by integrating peak area and comparing with internal standard peak area

steps.description: In the routine analysis, a working standard that was a mixture of C 46 GTGT and the GDGTs extracted and purified from East China Sea sediment was inserted every 20 samples to monitor ionization efficiency changes.
steps.searchable_summary: Insert a working standard every 20 samples to monitor ionization efficiency changes

steps.description: The correction value of ionization efficiency between GDGTs and the internal standard was obtained in our laboratory by comparing the peak areas of isolated crenarchaeol and branched GDGTs I and II (Schouten et al., 2013) and C 46 GTGT in known amounts.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain ionization efficiency correction value by comparing peak areas of isolated standards in known amounts
inputs: Isolated standards (crenarchaeol, branched GDGTs I, II, C46 GTGT)
